steps.title: Substitute x = 0 to find the initial value
steps.content:
The equation $y = 420 - 136x$ gives remaining distance $y$ (in meters) and time $x$ (in hours). At the start of the experiment, $x = 0$.

Substituting:
$$y = 420 - 136(0) = 420$$
The remaining distance at the start is 420 meters.

steps.title: Identify 420 as the y-intercept and interpret it
steps.content:
In a linear equation $y = mx + b$, the constant term $b$ is the y-intercept, the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Here, 420 is the y-intercept.

The coefficient -136 represents the rate of change: the rover covers 136 meters per hour. So 136 is the rover's speed, not 420.

Since 420 is the remaining distance at the start of the experiment, it represents the rover's initial distance from its target. That confirms C is correct.
